data.name: LinuxSerial.Manual
data.value:
To access the serial port {0}, you have several options:
Option 1 - Temporary access (until device is reconnected): sudo chmod 666 {0}
Option 2 - Permanent access (recommended): sudo usermod -aG dialout {1} Then log out and log back in
Option 3 - Automatic via udev rule: Create /etc/udev/rules.d/50-serial.rules: KERNEL=="ttyUSB[0-9]*", MODE="0666" KERNEL=="ttyACM[0-9]*", MODE="0666" Then run: sudo udevadm control --reload-rules

data.name: LinuxNetBind.Manual
